title: Meredith Quill
content:
{{CharHub|Meredith Quill|Meredith Quill}} {{Character |image = Meredith Quill Infobox.png |real name = Meredith Quill{{Ref|GotG}} |alias = {{Alias |derivatives = Mer{{Ref|GotG}} |nicknames = River Lily{{Ref|GotGV2}}}} |species = [[Human]] |citizenship = {{Citizenship|USA}} |gender = Female |DOB = [[1961]][http://waltdisneystudiosawards.com/media/scripts/GotG2.pdf ''Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2'' script][https://web.archive.org/web/20210925013246/https://twitter.com/JamesGunn/status/1411475065632215046 James Gunn on Twitter - July 3, 2021] |DOD = [[1988|Late 1988]]{{Ref|GotG}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20220430200723/https://twitter.com/JamesGunn/status/863882306431328257 James Gunn on Twitter - May 14, 2017] |movie = {{Infobox List|''[[Guardians of the Galaxy (film)|Guardians of the Galaxy]]''
''[[Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2]]''
{{Collapse|''[[Avengers: Infinity War]]'' (mentioned)
''[[Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3]]'' (photo)}}}} |tv movie = {{Infobox List|{{Collapse|''[[The Guardians of the Galaxy Holiday Special]]'' (mentioned)}}}} |comic = {{Infobox List|''[[Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2 Prelude]]''
{{Collapse|''[[Avengers: Endgame Prelude]]'' (mentioned)}}}} |book = {{Infobox List|{{Collapse|''[[Guardians of the Galaxy Read-Along Storybook and CD]]'' (mentioned)}}}} |actor = [[Laura Haddock]] |status = Deceased}} {{Quote|[[Peter Quill|Peter]], I know these last few months have been hard for you. But I’m going to a better place and I will be okay. And I will always be with you. You are the light of my life. My precious son. My little Star-Lord.|Meredith Quill|Guardians of the Galaxy (film)}} '''Meredith Quill''' was the mother of [[Peter Quill]], whom she had conceived with [[Ego]]. When Peter was eight, she died of brain cancer that Ego covertly implanted in her head. ==Biography== ===Meeting Ego=== {{Quote|You’re so like your [[Ego|daddy]], you even look like him. And he was an angel, composed outta pure light...|Meredith Quill to [[Peter Quill]]|Guardians of the Galaxy (film)}} [[File:Meredith Quill & Ego (King Cobra - 1980).png|{{AltFloat}}|Quill singing a song in a car ride with [[Ego]]]] Meredith was born to [[Jason Quill]]{{Ref|GotGV3}} and his [[Peter Quill's Grandmother|wife]] and raised in [[St. Charles]], [[Missouri]]. Growing up, she developed a strong love for pop music, and memorized songs that she heard on the radio. She eventually met [[Ego]], a man from space with whom she fell in love. In [[1980|1980]], Ego drove her out to a forest where he showed her a seedling he had planted, claiming that it would soon spread everywhere across [[Earth]]. [[File:Meredith & Peter Quill.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Quill spending quality time with her [[Star-Lord|son]]]] Meredith was soon impregnated by Ego{{Ref|GotGV2}} and later that year, when she was nineteen, gave birth to a son she named [[Peter Quill|Peter Jason Quill]],{{Ref|GotG}} giving him her father's first name as a middle name.{{Ref|GotGV3}} Ego was soon forced to return to his planet so he could replenish his life force. Meredith raised Peter by herself and gave him a [[Star-Lord's Walkman|Walkman]] with a mixtape of her favorite songs and dubbed him her "little Star-Lord."{{Ref|GotG}} ===Brain Cancer=== {{Quote|I returned to [[Earth]] to see her three times and I knew if I returned a fourth, I'd never leave. [[Ego's Expansion|Expansion]], the reason for my very existence would be over so I did what I had to do. But... it broke my heart to put that tumor in her head.|[[Ego]] to [[Star-Lord]]|Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2}} [[Ego]] returned three times to visit Meredith Quill. Fearing that he was becoming too attached to her and becoming unfocused in his mission, Ego decided to kill her, although he was not without remorse for it. He used his power to cause an inoperable malignancy to develop in her brain, dooming her to die of brain cancer.{{Ref|GotGV2}} ====Saying Goodbye==== [[File:Meredith Quill 6.png|{{AltFloat}}|Quill saying goodbye to her [[Peter Quill|son]]]] {{Quote|Your [[Jason Quill|grandpa]] is gonna take such good care of you. At least until your [[Ego|daddy]] comes back to get you... Take my hand. Peter? Take my hand.|Meredith Quill to [[Peter Quill]]|Guardians of the Galaxy (film)}} By [[1988]], when [[Peter Quill|Peter]] was eight years old, Meredith was on her deathbed. As her family gathered around her in her last moments, she spoke to Peter about the fight she'd heard he'd had at school. She then gave him a present consisting of a second mixtape of music, which she told him to open after she was gone. She then asked Peter to take her hand, but Peter refused. Meredith then passed away seconds after imploring Peter to take her hand, much to Peter's regret.{{Ref|GotG}} ===Legacy=== [[File:GofGv2 Merediths Memorial.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|[[Peter Quill|Peter]] gazing at Meredith Quill's statue]] {{Quote|My mother told everyone my father was from the stars. She had brain cancer, so everyone thought she was delusional.|[[Star-Lord]]|Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2}} {{Rewrite}} Many years later, after [[Battle of Xandar|defending]] the planet [[Xandar]] as a member of the [[Guardians of the Galaxy]], [[Peter Quill]] began hallucinating and saw an image of his dying mother asking him to take his hand when [[Gamora]] asked him to take her hand.{{Ref|GotG}} A statue of Meredith Quill was built on [[Ego]]'s planet. [[Peter Quill]] stated that his family back on [[Earth]] thought she was delusional due to her brain cancer when she claimed her husband was from the stars. Quill and Ego both got into a heated argument about why Ego never returned to see Meredith, to which Ego explained that his humanoid form must return to his planet regularly or it would decay and that he could not stand Meredith's death, claiming Quill could not understand how this felt. Ego stated that, despite him making countless mistakes for eons, Quill was not one of them and that he wished to be a father who bonded with his son, stating it was what Meredith wished for. He taught Quill to focus on his essence within the planet to harness energy to conjure up an energy ball, thus resulting in a bonding moment between father and son. However, this all went downhill when Peter learned about Ego's true motives and that Ego was the one who put the tumor inside Meredith's head in the first place. Angered by this revelation and determined to avenge his mother's death, as well as to protect the planets Ego had visited from the [[Ego's Expansion|Expansion]], Peter arranged for [[Rocket]] and [[Groot]] to plant a bomb inside Ego's core, destroying Ego for good.{{Ref|GotGV2}} ==Relationships== ===Family=== *[[Jason Quill]] - Father *[[Peter Quill's Grandmother|Mother]] † *[[List of Minor Characters/Phase Two#Guardians of the Galaxy|Brother]] *[[Peter Quill]] - Son ===Allies=== *[[Ego]] † - Former Lover and Killer *[[Higgins]] *Best Friend *[[Fitzgibbon (Doctor)|Fitzgibbon]] - Doctor ==Appearances== In chronological order: *{{Appearance|GotGV2}} (flashbacks) *{{Appearance|GotG}} (flashbacks) **{{Appearance|GotGV2P1}} (flashbacks) **{{Appearance|GotGV2P2}} (vision) **{{Appearance|GotGRASaCD}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|AIW}} (mentioned) **{{Appearance|AEP1}} (mentioned) **{{Appearance|AEP2}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|TGotGHS}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|GotGV3}} (photo) ==Trivia== *In the comics, Meredith Quill rescued the space prince [[w:c:marvel:J'son|J'son]] and fell in love with him. After his departure, she was killed by two [[Badoon]] soldiers, who were sent to kill their son [[Peter Quill|Peter]]. ==Behind the Scenes== *[[Heather Witherill]] was a stand-in for [[Laura Haddock]] in the role of Meredith Quill. ==References== {{Gallery|Meredith Quill/Gallery}} {{Reflist}} ==External Links== *{{Marvel}} [[Category:Characters]] [[Category:Guardians of the Galaxy (film) Characters]] [[Category:Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2 Characters]] [[Category:Comic Characters]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Blond Hair]] [[Category:Bald Hair]] [[Category:Green Eyes]] [[Category:Deceased Characters]] [[Category:Characters Killed by Ego]]
